Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement host-relay MCP bridge with security hardening

Full implementation of the host-relay system — a lightweight bridge that
lets AI agents in sandboxed environments execute simple shell commands on
the host machine via file-based IPC and the Model Context Protocol.

Components:
- hr/policy.py: Quote-aware command policy validator
- hr/spool.py: ULID-based job/result file I/O with atomic writes
- hr/worker.py: Job execution with Popen for graceful SIGTERM→SIGKILL
- hr/watcher.py: Thread pool watcher with fcntl locking
- hr/mcp_server.py: FastMCP server (host_run, spool_status, abort_job)
- hr/main.py: CLI (hr start/status/stop/mcp/--once)
- hr/env_resolver.py: Login shell env sourcing with dangerous key blocking
- install.sh: One-line cross-platform installer

Security hardening (from Opus 4.6 audit):
- Block BASH_ENV, LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES + 20 more env keys
- Reject $VAR, ${VAR}, $'...' ANSI-C quoting in commands
- Block wrapper commands (env, nice, sudo, awk, strace, etc.)
- Clamp job timeout to 120s max in worker
- Validate job IDs against ULID pattern (path traversal prevention)
- Skip symlinks and oversized files in spool
- Quarantine malformed job files instead of infinite retry
- Resolve redirect paths to catch ~/../../ etc traversal

149 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: code review remediation plan (phases 1–6) (#17)

* fix: phase 1 — immediate fixes from code review

- cap inspector notices array at 200 to prevent memory leak in long dev sessions
- add fail_ci_if_error flag to Codecov upload step so coverage regressions block CI
- add console.error fallback when persistence flush fails without custom onError handler
- guard store state() return value to be a plain object with descriptive error
- fix 6 markdown line-length violations causing docs:build failure (MD013)

* fix: phase 2 — correctness fixes from code review

- Fix isStateEqual() circular-reference false positive: visited.has()
  now returns false (conservative) instead of true in both array and
  object branches, so circular refs don't suppress mutation notifications
- Add try-catch + rollback to FSM send(): if the _enter hook throws,
  current is restored to the previous state to keep the FSM consistent
- Extend release.yml tarball validation: assert inspector/index.js and
  persistence/plugin.svelte.js are present; assert no .spec. files leak
- Add .nvmrc (24) to document the recommended Node version for CI parity
- Add engines.node >=20 to package.json to communicate consumer runtime
  requirements

* refactor: phase 3 — type safety improvements from code review

- Add src/lib/internal/marker-helpers.ts with readMarker/writeMarker
  helpers to encapsulate the double-cast pattern for symbol-keyed
  markers, eliminating 4x `as unknown as Record<symbol, X>` usages
- Refactor runtime/store-shell.svelte.ts, runtime/subscriptions.ts, and
  async/plugin.svelte.ts to use readMarker/writeMarker for the
  ASYNC_ACTION_MARKER symbol

* test: phase 4 — additional coverage from code review

- Add src/lib/ssr-and-dispose.spec.ts covering:
  - Manager-level isolation (simulates per-request SSR safety)
  - Plain-object guard error path
  - $dispose stops $subscribe and $onAction notifications
  - $dispose is idempotent (no throw on double-dispose)
  - Plugin onDispose callback fires exactly once
  - Error propagation from synchronous and async actions via $onAction
- Add FSM _enter rollback regression test to fsm.spec.ts: asserts
  current rolls back to the pre-transition state when _enter throws

* docs: phase 5 — documentation improvements from code review

- Document TimeTravelController API: entries, currentIndex, isReplaying,
  goTo() with a member-level table in both plugins.md and types.md
- Clarify pick/omit mutual exclusivity: add note that combining both
  throws at store registration time
- Document TTL + migrate interaction: TTL expiry is evaluated before
  migrate; expired payloads skip migration and fall back to initial state
- Expand sync conflict policy description: explicitly call it
  last-write-wins and note that it is deterministic but not causally
  consistent, with guidance for custom merge strategies
- Expand async concurrency policy descriptions to be more precise about
  each policy's behavior

* ci: phase 6 — CI hardening from code review

- Add 'pnpm audit --prod --audit-level=high' step to ci.yml to catch
  production dependency vulnerabilities on every CI run
- Add 'pnpm exec publint --strict ./dist' step to ci.yml to enforce
  strict publish readiness after every build
- Add version-match assertion to release.yml: block the release job
  when the git tag does not match the version in package.json

* fix: address all four review comments from code review

- ci.yml: add set -o pipefail so tee pipe cannot mask build failures
- ssr-and-dispose.spec.ts: rewrite onDispose test to use createStoreShell
  directly, fixing inaccurate comment and proving exactly-once semantics
  on double-dispose
- docs/reference/types.md: clarify $timeTravel.goTo() mutates live state
  (not truly read-only)
- docs/reference/plugins.md: clarify $timeTravel read-only means
  no new history entries recorded, not no state mutation"

Remove service principal credentials (AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID)
from App Service settings. Set AZURE_CLIENT_ID to the user-assigned managed
identity client ID so DefaultAzureCredential uses ManagedIdentityCredential
instead of EnvironmentCredential. No secrets to rotate.

productionize circadian k into advise_correction_isf()

Add _calibrate_circadian_k() that splits corrections into day (06-22) and
night (22-06) windows, calibrating separate k values. Population defaults:
day k=2.2, night k=3.8 (from EXP-2588). advise_correction_isf() now uses
circadian-weighted overall_k and includes circadian note in evidence.

All 348 tests pass.

commit.message:
fix: inventory + button uses data-model approach to ensure row is visible (sd-s59)

Switch inv-add-btn click handler from direct DOM append to
data-model pattern (matching combat spell list):
- Push new item to gear.inventory model
- Save via saveGear()
- Re-render list from model (same code path as page-load render)
- Scroll new row into view

This guarantees the row appears regardless of any CSS or browser
rendering edge case, and satisfies the persist-on-reload criterion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: inventory + button uses data-model approach to ensure row is visible (sd-s59)

Switch inv-add-btn click handler from direct DOM append to
data-model pattern (matching combat spell list):
- Push new item to gear.inventory model
- Save via saveGear()
- Re-render list from model (same code path as page-load render)
- Scroll new row into view

This guarantees the row appears regardless of any CSS or browser
rendering edge case, and satisfies the persist-on-reload criterion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show total elapsed time from scan start when reconnecting

When reconnecting to a running experiment via polling, the elapsed time
now shows the TOTAL duration from when the scan originally started, not
from when polling started.

Changes:
- /api/scans/<repo> endpoint now returns 'running_experiment_created_at'
  with the directory creation timestamp (when scan started)
- reconnectToRunningExperiment() accepts createdAt parameter
- Elapsed time calculated from actual scan start time using Date objects
- Progress updates show accurate 'elapsed Xm Ys' since start
- Total time on completion is now correct

Benefits:
- Users see realistic progress (e.g., 'elapsed 2m 15s' if scan ran
  that long before browser disconnected)
- Better understanding of total duration vs remaining time
- Accurate timing for 7-minute timeout threshold

Example flow:
- Scan starts at 10:00
- Browser disconnects at 10:02 (2 min elapsed)
- Browser reconnects at 10:05
- UI shows 'elapsed 5m Xs' (total since 10:00)
- Not 'elapsed 3m Xs' (time from reconnect)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:

exp: EXP-2588 Circadian counter-regulation — night k higher CONFIRMED

H1 CONFIRMED: Night k is systematically higher than day k
  Mean Δk (night-day) = +1.5, median = +0.8
  6/12 night higher, 2/12 day higher, 4/12 similar
  Most dramatic: patients a,d both show day k=1.0, night k=7.0

H2 CONFIRMED: Night corrections have stronger counter-regulation
  Consistent with dawn phenomenon / overnight HGP
  Sim overestimates correction drops more at night

Practical implication: forward sim should use time-specific k:
  Day (06-22): lower k (mean 3.1, median 2.2)
  Night (22-06): higher k (mean 4.5, median 3.8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

exp: EXP-2587 TIR-based counter-reg k prediction — NOT CONFIRMED

All hypotheses NOT CONFIRMED:
  H1: Best single-feature LOO MAE=1.16 (TIR), barely beats
      population median k=1.5 (MAE=1.21). Not < 1.0 threshold.
  H2: TIR correlation r=-0.539 exists but LOO prediction is unstable.
  H3: Multi-feature models overfit catastrophically (14 patients too few).

Key finding: cannot shortcut correction-based k calibration with glucose
metrics. Population median k=1.5 remains the best fallback when
< MIN_CORRECTIONS available for direct calibration.

Validates the design in settings_advisor: direct correction calibration
is essential, population k=1.5 fallback is optimal for cold start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(migrate/registrar): eliminate dead None-arm branch in register_skips_already_registered

The `if let Some(content) = written` at line 190 of the test created a
branch whose None arm was unreachable: `register_plugins` always writes
the marketplace file even when all entries are already registered, so
`get_written` never returns None.

Replace the guarded `if let` with direct `expect()` calls (permitted in
tests per `clippy.toml`) to eliminate the dead branch and strengthen the
assertion — the test now fails explicitly if the file wasn't written.

Branch coverage: 95.45% → 95.50%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clear placeholder text when reconnecting to running scan

When a user selects a repo and auto-reconnects to a running experiment,
or clicks 'Watch Running Scan' from the modal, the initial placeholder
text ('Scan output will appear here...') was still visible.

Now the placeholder is cleared when reconnecting, so users see:

- Any past output that was preserved in the DOM
- Real-time updates as polling continues

This prevents the confusing UX where both the placeholder message
and the actual scan output appear together.

commit.message:
Fix: Ensure scan processes survive browser disconnect

Add 'start_new_session=True' to subprocess.Popen() call. This creates
the scan process in a new process group, ensuring it continues running
even if:
- The HTTP request is closed
- The user navigates away from the page
- The user refreshes the page
- The Flask server receives SIGTERM

Without this, the process would be terminated when the parent Flask
worker thread exits, killing the scan even though it was designed to
be server-persistent.

This is critical for background scans that should complete regardless
of browser connection status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prevent duplicate polling loops when reconnecting to running scan

Track polling interval in global variable currentPollInterval so we can
cancel any existing polling before starting a new one. This fixes the issue
where reconnecting multiple times would create multiple polling loops,
causing duplicate log messages.

Changes:
- Add currentPollInterval global variable
- Clear existing polling before starting new polling loop
- Set currentPollInterval to null when polling completes or times out
- Clear polling when user clicks reset button

This ensures only one polling loop runs at a time, preventing:
- Duplicate reconnect messages
- Duplicate progress updates
- Out-of-order elapsed time messages
- Resource waste from multiple simultaneous polling loops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace browser confirm() with custom modal for scan options

Replace browser's default confirm() dialog with custom modal that has
clear, actionable buttons:
- '👁 Watch Running Scan' (primary green button)
- '🔄 Start New Scan' (secondary button)
- '✕ Cancel' (danger button)

Benefits:
- Better UX with descriptive button labels instead of OK/Cancel
- Styled to match app theme (dark mode, orange accents)
- More mobile-friendly
- Shows experiment ID in message
- Easier to understand the choice being made

Added:
- Modal HTML structure in index.html
- Modal CSS styles (dark theme, hover effects, responsive)
- showScanModal() function to display and handle modal
- Event handlers for each button action

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Dynamic API base URL resolution and user creation workflow (#6)

Introduce dynamic API base URL resolution and proxy support for enhanced
deployment flexibility. Add a user creation feature with accessibility
and internationalization improvements, including form validation and
user feedback. Enhance authentication robustness by expanding token
extraction methods. Improve branding with a favicon and streamline user
lookup interactions for better accessibility.

commit.message:
fix(dante): use matrix_proc.output_dsp for GR meters in inferno build

The inferno feature path referenced dsp_state (from the stub path) instead
of matrix_proc.output_dsp after the MatrixProcessor refactor. Caused compile
failure when building with --features patchbox-dante/inferno.

Fixes: device disappearing from Dante Controller after deployment.

fix: address review issues in navigation, slider, and clipboard changes

- Add OnNavigatingFrom/OnNavigatedTo lifecycle hooks to Window
- Clear TransitionManager and GlobalTweenEngine during navigation
- Remove Console.WriteLine from ApplyNavigation (library code)
- Revert ClampValue to consistent Math.Clamp(v, _min, _max)

- Add TransitionManager.Clear() method

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add battery level to snapshots and scooter summary

- Add battery INTEGER column to gt3_snapshots table with migration
- Include battery in INSERT, InfluxDB writePoint, and SELECT queries
- Return battery in scooterSummary for FluxHaus dashboard consumption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor search platform modules for improved documentation and structure (#49)

- Enhanced module documentation across various search platform files, including Reddit, Semantic Scholar, StackOverflow, Wikipedia, YouTube, and SearXNG, to clarify ownership boundaries, pipeline positions, key types, and invariants.
- Updated data structures in Reddit, StackOverflow, and YouTube modules to include public field documentation.
- Improved test functions in multiple modules to return `anyhow::Result<()>` for better error handling.
- Added new `.gitkeep` files in research fixture directories to maintain directory structure.
- Refined DDG parser test to ensure at least three HTTPS non-redirect results are returned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Bot <copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(tooling): Add prepare/restore modes for publish-readmes script

The readmes publish process previously accepted a single 'root' or 'sdk'
mode that could overwrite local README files and lacked a safe restore path.

The script now supports verb:target syntax (prepare:extension,
prepare:sdk, restore:extension, restore:sdk). It creates backups before
modifying README.md and restores them after publishing.

This preserves local README state and makes per-package publishing safer. The
targeted approach was chosen over a single 'root' mode because it avoids
accidental loss of local changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexandre Nicolaie <xunleii@users.noreply.github.com>
